summary: Cross-chapter audit of cycle 03 manuscript (19 chapters, 4560 prose lines, 111840 prose words, 8967 sentences). REDUNDANCY: The manuscript's primary redundancy pattern is re-description of known character traits through new observers (Kem's height/pale skin/signet re-inventoried in Ch7, Ch12, Ch15; Dren's scar re-described in full in Ch11 and Ch16). Margins settlement arrivals in Ch12 and Ch14 reuse the Ch7 arrival template. Proof-of-baseline accumulation is severe for Vel's Maren/threshold mission (Ch17 line 7629 recaps the entire 8-year arc without new leverage; the Maren journal's two-handwriting description repeats 5+ times). Dren's daughter Mira is re-proved as motivation 10+ times with only one instance (Ch16 line 6931) adding medical detail. Two chapter-boundary near-verbatim recaps exist (Ch8→Ch9 suturing instructions, Ch15→Ch16 travel logistics). Image density is dominated by hands/palms/fingers at 616 occurrences (32 per chapter), functioning as the manuscript's primary prose tic. Em-dash density (0.61%), sentence-opener monotony (no pronoun exceeds 7% manuscript-wide), and hedge-word density (all patterns below 1 per 800 words) are within acceptable thresholds. CONSISTENCY: One quantitative inconsistency found — Ch11 line 4973 states Kem was at 166 entering the Spent Towns, but the established canonical value is 225 (Ch7 line 2911). All other level tracking, object arcs, timeline, geography, and knowledge states are internally consistent across all 19 chapters.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch3 line 535: 'He was tall, maybe mid-twenties, with sunburn starting at the nose and ears...cream-and-gray Vault clothes were snagged and dirt-marked now, but expensive even ruined. His hands had no calluses.'; Ch7 line 2905: 'Their eyes caught on the height, the pale indoor skin that was only now starting to redden across his nose and forearms, the clothes that hadn't been mended because they'd never been worn long enough to need it.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch7 line 2905 re-describes three traits from Kem's Ch3 introduction (height, pale/reddening skin at the nose, unworn expensive clothes) through townspeople's eyes. The reader received this full physical portrait at Ch3 line 535. Though filtered through new observers, the descriptive function is identical: orienting the reader to Kem's appearance.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the townspeople-observation passage at line 2905. The settlement locals can register Kem as an outsider through one specific detail rather than re-inventorying three known traits. Keep the signet reaction at line 2937 which drives new plot.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch7's settlement scene identifies Kem as an outsider through no more than one physical trait the reader has not already received.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2 lines 438-440: 'The Aldraste signet was silver-dark, heavy, the crest a closed circle. Kem picked it up. Warm metal settled into his palm, heavier than it looked.'; Ch7 line 2933: 'His House signet sat on his finger, silver, heavy, carved with the Aldraste crest, the closed circle, the mark of preservation.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch7 line 2933 re-describes the signet with near-verbatim phrasing from Ch2 lines 438-440: both use 'silver'/'silver-dark', 'heavy', 'closed circle', 'Aldraste'. The signet has also been referenced in Ch3 line 535 and multiple times in Ch3-Ch4. By Ch7, the reader knows what the signet looks like.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the full physical re-description of the signet at line 2933 with a brief reference ('the signet' or 'the ring'). The keeper's reaction at line 2935 does not require the reader to be re-taught the object's appearance.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: The signet passage in Ch7 does not repeat 'silver', 'heavy', or 'closed circle' — terms already established in Ch2.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch9 lines 3831-3867: full stitching scene showing wound creation and Kem's bad stitches in real time; Ch11 line 4767: 'His left arm moved carefully. The silo wound had healed into a knotted ridge of scar that pulled tight when he extended the elbow, Kem's inexpert stitches leaving puckered seams in the skin like a badly mended seam in leather. The arm worked, but not easily.'; also Ch11 lines 4789 and 4793 describe the scar again.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch11 line 4767 provides a full physical re-inventory of Dren's scar just two chapters after the reader watched every stitch go in during Ch9. The 'knotted ridge', 'puckered seams', 'inexpert stitches', and 'badly mended seam' are all information the reader holds from the stitching scene. Additionally, lines 4789 and 4793 in the same chapter describe the scar twice more.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: At line 4767, reduce the scar description to a single functional detail (e.g., 'the arm that wouldn't straighten'). Cut or compress the two additional scar references at lines 4789 and 4793 to at most one brief touch.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 references Dren's arm injury no more than twice, and neither reference re-describes the scar's physical appearance in terms already shown during Ch9.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch11 line 4767: 'knotted ridge of scar that pulled tight when he extended the elbow'; Ch13 line 5492: 'left arm held a little away from his body the way he carried it when the old wound had started burning'; Ch16 line 6911: 'The silo gash had healed into a thick shiny ridge from wrist to elbow, and the hand on that side only half closed until he worked it awake.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch16 line 6911 provides another full re-description of Dren's scar ('thick shiny ridge from wrist to elbow, hand only half closed') after the reader has received full scar inventories in Ch11 and functional references in Ch12 and Ch13. This is the 4th detailed scar description since Ch9.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: At line 6911, replace the scar re-description with a single behavioral detail showing the arm's limitation (e.g., the pack adjustment) without re-inventorying the scar's physical appearance.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16's first reference to Dren's arm conveys functional limitation without re-describing the scar's appearance using 'ridge', 'gash', or similar terms already used in Ch9/Ch11.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 2881: 'cluster of buildings in a shallow valley...buildings were stone and timber, shaped but not uniform...Repairs showed as visible seams between ability-work and manual labor'; Ch12 line 5096: 'cluster of low buildings arranged around a central well, the structures showing the seams of cheap Shaping work. The walls met at angles that weren't quite right. Rooflines sagged where the original builder had run out of levels mid-construction.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch12 line 5096 uses the same settlement-arrival template as Ch7 line 2881: exterior cluster view, Shaping-seam architecture assessment, structural-quality inventory. The reader learned to read Shaping seams in Ch7. The Ch12 settlement is poorer, but the orienting function is identical.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Enter the Ch12 settlement through what distinguishes it from Ch7's (the chalk soil, the exhaustion, the human cost) rather than re-teaching the Shaping-seam architecture. One line of structural context is sufficient.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch12's settlement arrival does not re-describe Shaping construction quality as a general phenomenon. Entry foregrounds what is new about this place.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch12 line 5098: 'A woman sat outside...shelling dried beans...She looked up when they approached. Her eyes went to Kem first, to his height, his hands, the boots'; Ch14 line 5933: 'A woman with two sacks...watched Vel first, then Kem, then the distance between them. An old man by the wall...pretending he was not listening. Two boys...fell silent when they noticed Kem's boots.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch14 line 5927-5933 repeats the Margins-settlement-arrival template for the third time: exterior lean/collapse description, well as anchor, locals watching and measuring newcomers (specifically Kem's boots and appearance). Two chapters after Ch12 used the same structure.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Enter the Ch14 settlement through the specific geography (the crack, the lean, the retaining wall) rather than the generic outsider-assessment scene. Cut or compress the watching-locals inventory at line 5933.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14's settlement arrival does not include more than one observer assessing Kem's appearance.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch3 line 535: 'sunburn starting at the nose and ears'; multiple chapters tracking hand transformation; Ch15 line 6508: 'The man in front of her was sunburned, leaner than the painting, bruised along one forearm, and rough in the hands with work learned late and fast.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch15 line 6508 re-describes Kem through Sable's POV: 'sunburned' (established Ch3, restated Ch7) and 'rough in the hands' (tracked across Ch4, Ch7, Ch10). 'Leaner than the painting' is new Sable-specific information, but the other traits are reader-facing redundancy.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Keep 'leaner than the painting' which is new information. Replace 'sunburned' and 'rough in the hands with work learned late and fast' with details Sable uniquely notices from her institutional vantage point.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Sable's first observation of Kem does not repeat 'sunburned' or hand-roughness traits the narrative has already established through other POVs.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch3 line 535: 'He was tall'; Ch7 line 2905: 'Their eyes caught on the height'; Ch12 line 5098: 'Her eyes went to Kem first, to his height, his hands, the boots that were battered now but still identifiably Vault-made.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch12 line 5098 re-uses the external-observer-noticing-Kem's-height pattern for the third time (after Ch3 line 535 and Ch7 line 2905). The assessment structure — stranger looks at Kem, registers height, then secondary details — mirrors Ch7's townspeople scene.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the height reference at line 5098. Let the woman's assessment land on one detail specific to the Margins context rather than repeating the height-first assessment structure.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: The bean-shelling woman's assessment of Kem does not reference his height.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1 line 89: 'Eight years ago she had watched a door open at zero and Maren go through it. Since then every private calculation had bent toward the Scour.'; Ch17 line 7629: 'Somewhere down there, eight years ago, Maren had reached zero. The threshold had opened, shown a flash of somewhere else, and closed. Vel had watched. She had been watching ever since, from sixty-two levels, from thirty-one, from twenty, from fourteen, burning herself down across eight years to follow a woman through a door that might never open twice.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch17 line 7629 recaps Vel's entire eight-year mission arc — the crossing, the eight years of pursuit, every level milestone — without adding any new information, leverage, or consequence. This baseline was established at Ch1 line 89 and expanded with vivid new detail at Ch16 lines 7103-7129 (the full Maren story). Line 7629 is pure atmospheric restatement of facts the reader already holds.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or radically compress line 7629. The reader has just heard the full Maren story in Ch16. Arriving at the Scour ridge needs one sentence of emotional registration, not a recapitulation of the entire arc.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch17's opening passage at the ridgeline does not restate Vel's level history or the eight-year timeline. It registers the arrival without recapping what the reader already knows.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch10 line 4188: 'Maren's handwriting ran slanted and impatient along the top margin. Vel's newer notes sat underneath in smaller blockier script'; Ch12 line 5536: 'Vel sat with Maren's route journal open on her knees...The handwriting on the open spread wasn't hers'; Ch14 line 6295: 'Vel had Maren's route journal open on her knees. Kem had already noticed the two handwritings days ago. Tonight the neater one stopped halfway down a distance column.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch14 line 6295 is the 4th description of Maren's journal as having two contrasting handwritings (after Ch1 line 323, Ch10 line 4188, Ch12 line 5536). The journal-on-knees posture and the two-handwriting observation serve the same orienting function each time. The reader knows who wrote what.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: At line 6295, drop the handwriting observation. Enter the journal through what Kem is reading or what information Vel is extracting, not the physical fact of two handwritings.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14's journal reference does not describe two handwritings or the contrast between Maren's and Vel's script.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch10 line 4188: 'Maren's handwriting ran slanted'; Ch16 line 7407: 'Maren's smaller slanted script crowded the margins around later additions in Vel's flatter hand'; Ch17 line 7795: 'Maren's handwriting crowded the margins with wind notes, rock notes, a small x at the cascade site'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch17 line 7795 is the 6th or 7th description of Maren's handwriting in the journal margins. The 'slanted/crowded margins' pattern has been used at Ch1 line 323, Ch10 line 4188, Ch10 line 4534, Ch14 line 6295, Ch16 line 7407, and now Ch17 line 7795. Each repetition re-proves the same baseline: Maren wrote in this journal and her handwriting is distinct.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: At line 7795, drop the handwriting description. If the passage needs emotional weight, anchor it in Vel's reaction to what is written (the word 'here'), not in the physical appearance of script the reader has seen described 5+ times.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch17's journal passage references the content of Maren's notes without re-describing her handwriting style.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 3029: 'My girl. Mira. Her legs are —' (first establishment); Ch9 line 4075: 'Dren had a daughter. Dren had route math.' (3rd restatement); Ch14 line 6203: 'Fear for Mira stayed where he always kept it, under the practical layer, under the jokes, under the daily arithmetic of not saying aloud what waited north if he failed to get back in time.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch14 line 6203 is the 6th restatement of Dren's baseline motivation (sick daughter Mira, needs money for healer). By Ch14, the reader has been told this at Ch7 line 3029 (first), Ch7 line 3592 (location detail), Ch9 line 4075, Ch11 line 4987, and Ch12 line 5323. Line 6203 is pure atmospheric repetition — it adds no new detail about Mira's condition, Dren's situation, or the financial arithmetic.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut line 6203 or replace with a single-sentence behavioral note showing Dren's preoccupation rather than narratively restating the baseline the reader has received 5 times.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 does not include a narrator-summary of Dren's fear for Mira. Dren's motivation is shown through action or a single brief gesture, not an interior-state inventory.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch16 line 6931: 'My daughter's sick...her legs won't take her when she swings out of bed. She hits the floor and looks at me...' (first detailed medical description, legitimate); Ch16 line 7582: 'Mira. The healer. The whole awful arithmetic of it.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch16 line 7582 restates the Mira baseline ('Mira. The healer. The whole awful arithmetic of it.') from Kem's POV after Dren has already departed with the full, detailed payoff scene at line 6931. The payoff scene added new medical detail and emotional specificity. Line 7582, coming 650 lines later in the same chapter, reduces that detail back to atmospheric shorthand.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or rework line 7582. If Kem's reflection on Dren's departure needs to reference Mira, anchor it in a specific image from the departure scene (e.g., the way Dren carried his pack on one shoulder) rather than the abstract 'arithmetic' formula.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16's later reflection on Dren does not restate the Mira/healer/arithmetic baseline in summary form.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch8 line 3804: 'You're closing skin, not tying off a grain sack...Straight through. Both sides, same depth. Pull it snug, not tight.'; Ch9 line 3837: 'Straight through...Both sides, same depth. Pull it snug, not tight. You're closing skin. Not tying off a sack.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch9 line 3837 repeats Vel's suturing instructions near-verbatim from Ch8 line 3804. Approximately 17 content words appear in the same order across the chapter boundary. Ch9 opens by re-narrating the instructions the reader just received at the end of Ch8, including the creek-rescue summary at line 3833 which also echoes Ch8 line 3652.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the repeated suturing instructions at Ch9 line 3837. Ch9 can open with the needle going in wrong (line 3825) without re-quoting instructions the reader received 33 lines earlier. The creek-rescue summary at line 3833 can also be compressed — the reader was there.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch9's opening does not re-quote the 'straight through, both sides, same depth, pull it snug not tight' instruction from Ch8.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch15 line 6882: '...still about two days from the basin, because cover cost time and wounded men cost more.'; Ch16 line 6899: 'They were still about two days from the basin because they'd spent two days not going straight at it.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch16 line 6899 recaps the travel logistics from Ch15 line 6882 at the chapter boundary. The 8-word phrase 'still about two days from the basin because' appears in both, and both passages explain why the direct road was not taken. The reader received this information 17 lines earlier.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Ch16 should open with the present situation (Dren packing, the relay flashes) rather than recapping Ch15's travel summary. Cut or compress line 6899's logistics paragraph — the reader knows they're two days out.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16's opening paragraph does not restate the 'two days from the basin' distance or re-explain why the direct road was not taken.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Manuscript-wide: 616 occurrences of hands/palms/fingers across 19 chapters (32.4 per chapter average). Ch5 (lines 1829-2351): 51 occurrences in 260 prose lines = 1 hand/palm/finger reference every 5 prose lines.
redundancy_findings.problem: Hands/palms/fingers are the manuscript's primary prose tic at 616 total occurrences. Ch5 has 51 references in 260 prose lines, the highest density in the novel. The body defaults to hands for every physical beat, emotional registration, and moment of hesitation. At 20+ occurrences, the target is keeping at most 8-10 per chapter.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch5, audit every hand/palm/finger reference and cut or replace at least 60% of them. Keep hand references that are load-bearing (e.g., Kem's re-opened palm, Vel handing equipment) and cut atmospheric or habitual ones (e.g., 'his hand drifted toward', 'her hands were').
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch5 contains no more than 20 hand/palm/finger references, with cuts focused on atmospheric rather than functional uses.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch8 (lines 3138-3822): 44 hand/palm/finger references in 339 prose lines. Manuscript-wide total: 616.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch8 has 44 hand references in 339 prose lines, well above the 32.4 per chapter average. The silo fight and aftermath drive some functional hand references, but the density suggests many atmospheric uses that could be varied.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Audit hand references in Ch8 and cut or diversify at least 50% of non-functional ones. Vary the body parts used for physical beats — posture, jaw, shoulders, feet can carry some of this weight.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 contains no more than 22 hand/palm/finger references.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch13 (lines 5476-5922): 42 hand/palm/finger references in 222 prose lines. Manuscript-wide total: 616.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch13 has 42 hand references in only 222 prose lines — the highest density ratio in the novel at roughly 1 hand reference every 5.3 lines. The road-collapse rescue and spending sequence account for some, but the density exceeds functional need.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Audit hand references in Ch13 and cut at least 50%. The road-collapse scene can work through whole-body descriptions (balance, weight, stance) rather than defaulting to hands for every physical action.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 contains no more than 20 hand/palm/finger references.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch19 (lines 8735-9225): 44 hand/palm/finger references in 244 prose lines. Manuscript-wide total: 616.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch19 has 44 hand references in 244 prose lines, the highest density ratio in the novel after Ch13. The resolution chapter defaults to hands for nearly every physical and emotional beat.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Audit hand references in Ch19 and cut at least 50%. The reunion and resolution scenes can use a wider range of physical details (voice, posture, gaze, proximity).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch19 contains no more than 20 hand/palm/finger references.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch19 lines 8909+8913: 'I'm glad you're alive.' / 'I'm not ready for the rest of it,' Maren said.; Ch19 line 9157: 'I'm glad you're alive. I'm not ready for the rest of it, so don't push.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Within Ch19, Maren's two key sentences ('I'm glad you're alive' and 'I'm not ready for the rest of it') appear near-verbatim at lines 8909/8913 and again at line 9157. The 15-word near-exact repetition within a single chapter undermines the emotional weight of both instances.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one instance and rework the other. If the first (8909/8913) is the stronger dramatic moment, line 9157 should reference the earlier exchange without quoting it ('She said the same thing again, or close enough').
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: The exact phrases 'I'm glad you're alive' and 'I'm not ready for the rest of it' each appear only once in Ch19.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 2911: 'His Ledger pulsed at the edge of his awareness: 225.'; Ch11 line 4973: 'One hundred and sixty-six when they'd entered the Spent Towns. One hundred and sixty after the escape. One hundred and forty-eight now.'
consistency_findings.problem: Ch11 line 4973 states Kem was at level 166 when entering the Spent Towns. The canonical and manuscript-established value is 225 (Ch7 line 2911). The number 166 does not correspond to any tracked spending event. The sequence '166 → 160 → 148' at line 4973 implies only 6 levels were spent during the escape from Thessa's settlement, but the spend-by-spend accounting in Ch10 shows a much larger drop from 183 (post-silo fight) to 160.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Correct line 4973. Replace 'One hundred and sixty-six when they'd entered the Spent Towns' with the canonical Spent Towns entry level (225) or, if the retrospective is meant to reference a different milestone, use the correct level for that point (e.g., 183 before the Ch10 escape). Verify the arithmetic of the three-number descent described in this passage.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: The retrospective level-count at Ch11 line 4973 uses numbers that match the established spend-by-spend tracking from prior chapters.
